prompt:
Let $m \neq 0$ be an integer. Find all polynomials $P(x)$ with real coefficients such that
$$
\left(x^{3}-m x^{2}+1\right) P(x+1)+\left(x^{3}+m x^{2}+1\right) P(x-1)=2\left(x^{3}-m x+1\right) P(x)
$$
for all real numbers $x$.
response_text:
To find all polynomials $P(x)$ with real coefficients that satisfy the given equation:
$$ \left(x^{3}-m x^{2}+1\right) P(x+1)+\left(x^{3}+m x^{2}+1\right) P(x-1)=2\left(x^{3}-m x+1\right) P(x) $$
we can rewrite it by grouping the terms:
$$ (x^3+1)[P(x+1)+P(x-1)-2P(x)] - mx^2[P(x+1)-P(x-1)] + 2mxP(x) = 0 $$

Let $d$ be the degree of the polynomial $P(x)$, and let its leading term be $a_d x^d$ (with $a_d \neq 0$). 
We can analyze the leading terms of each part of the equation:
1. $P(x+1)+P(x-1)-2P(x)$ has a leading term of $d(d-1)a_d x^{d-2}$. Multiplied by $(x^3+1)$, the leading term is $d(d-1)a_d x^{d+1}$.
2. $P(x+1)-P(x-1)$ has a leading term of $2d a_d x^{d-1}$. Multiplied by $-mx^2$, the leading term is $-2md a_d x
